votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the logical equivalence defined in the Coq specification by checking if the boolean result matches the disjunction of the Pythagorean conditions.
The Python code correctly implements the iff condition by checking if the boolean result matches the evaluation of the Pythagorean theorem for the given sides.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the boolean result matches the Pythagorean theorem condition for any combination of the three sides.

The Python code correctly checks whether the result boolean matches the condition that one of the three Pythagorean relationships holds (a²+b²=c², a²+c²=b², or b²+c²=a²). The Coq spec states result=true iff one of these conditions holds, which is exactly what the Python code verifies by checking bool(result) == is_right. Both use arbitrary precision integers (Z in Coq, Python ints), so arithmetic matches on all concrete inputs.
